title: Keelhouse
subtitle: lean macOS agent cockpit for Jason's real workflow — real CLI agents and composer first, with resizable explorer/editor/browser trays, without IDE bloat
loop: Open project folder -> talk to one or more real Claude/Codex/Gemini CLI agents in the main cockpit -> resize or move trays to inspect/edit files or preview web output -> switch projects without separate heavy VS Code windows. Build order is dependency-first: finish chrome/trays, add search/command leverage, measure daily-driver value and resource use, then settings/AI hooks, transcripts/notifications/worktrees, source-host integrations, packaging, and speculative expansion.

cards.title: Reference product audit
cards.summary: Lock which external products are useful references so Keelhouse does not drift into a VS Code clone or generic orchestrator.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: Zed is the closest chrome-quality reference, but Keelhouse must translate that craft into an agent-first cockpit instead of copying an editor-first IDE. From awesome-agent-orchestrators, ConstellAgent, herdr, parallel-code, and Clave are the closest targeted references. Orbit is useful for agent workflow mechanics, not shell chrome. See docs/reference-products.md.

cards.title: Promote app into real root/package
cards.summary: Turn the proven spike into the actual app package: stable naming, app identifier, scripts, dev/build paths, and repo layout. Stop treating the working terminal loop as a side experiment.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: app builds/runs from app/; npm package, Rust crate, and Tauri product identifier are renamed; README/ROADMAP point to app/; runtime smoke launched target/debug/agent-cli and spawned claude in the selected cwd.

cards.title: Native open-folder workspace
cards.summary: Native folder picker creates the current workspace, matching the actual VS Code workflow: open app, pick Project A, terminal starts in Project A.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: first launch opens a native dialog; selected folder becomes the app workspace; terminal cwd matches it; Cmd+O switches folders.

cards.title: Launch real Claude in selected folder
cards.summary: Replace the current hardcoded login shell with a real agent process launched in the selected workspace. Start with Claude as the v0 default because it is the primary observed workflow.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: after folder selection/relaunch, one pane launches `claude` in that cwd from a persisted launch profile; Claude's TUI renders in the canvas.

cards.title: Persist and reopen last workspace
cards.summary: Quit/relaunch should not make the user pick the same project again. Store the last workspace path in a small local persistence layer.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08 for the current shell-pane default: relaunch reopens the last folder and starts the pane there. Recheck after AGENT-LAUNCH changes the default command.

cards.title: Terminal component hardening pass
cards.summary: Close the remaining gaps between 'spike works' and 'can daily-drive one agent': resize behavior, scrollback basics, keyboard audit, copy/selection edge cases, and fast-output smoke tests.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: Ghostty viewport scrollback renders, Shift+PageUp/PageDown and wheel scrolling work, resize clamps dimensions, 300-line fast output stays usable, and copy-selection still works.

cards.title: Agent launch env and auth checks
cards.summary: Claude/Codex must launch with the same PATH/auth expectations as a normal terminal. Detect missing binaries, auth failures, and bad cwd before dumping the user into a dead pane.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: workspace cwd and command are preflighted before pane replacement; missing command renders a visible error with no child process; process exit emits a banner for runtime/auth-style failures; Claude still launches as a direct child in the selected cwd.

cards.title: Local state storage and reset
cards.summary: Define where workspace/session state lives and how to reset it when the app gets confused.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: docs/local-state.md documents the Tauri Store path, workspace.json schema, reversible reset command, and default launchProfile repair path.

cards.title: Lean app shell: rail/editor/terminal layout
cards.summary: Build the shape that replaces VS Code for this workflow: left file rail, main editor area, bottom/right terminal pane area. No marketing page, no extra IDE chrome.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: one project opens into a stable three-part shell with left file rail surface, editor surface, and bottom terminal panel; terminal resize uses the measured terminal panel; build/tests pass; real Tauri smoke launched Claude in the selected cwd.

cards.title: File explorer rail
cards.summary: The VS Code feature Jason actually uses: a project tree on the left. Keep it dense, fast, and boring.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: backend lists the selected workspace with ignore::WalkBuilder, gitignore/app noisy-folder filtering, and an 8000-entry cap; frontend renders a dense React Arborist tree, directories expand/collapse, and files activate into the editor surface.

cards.title: File watcher and ignore rules
cards.summary: The rail must reflect real project changes without drowning in node_modules, target, build output, or ignored files.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: debounced notify watcher emits workspace-tree-changed for relevant paths only; frontend refreshes the rail from list_workspace_tree; noisy paths are unit-tested; visual smoke showed a newly created top-level directory appear in the rail after launch.

cards.title: Recent projects switcher
cards.summary: Opening the same active projects repeatedly should be cheap, not a folder-picker ceremony every time.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: recentFolders persists, dedupes, caps, and prunes missing folders; the rail lists recent projects with an explicit Switch affordance; real Tauri smoke switched from Keelhouse to postures and persisted postures as the active folder.

cards.title: CodeMirror file editor
cards.summary: Open files from the rail into a real editor pane. This is not a decorative preview; it is the VS Code/Codex editor surface Jason actually uses.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: CodeMirror opens files from the rail with reliable pointer-down activation, line numbers, path context, TS/TSX/JS/JSX/MD syntax modes, dirty/saved status, Save button, and Cmd+S/Ctrl+S. Native UI smoke opened, edited, saved, and confirmed disk contents changed for a real workspace file.

cards.title: Robust editor parity UX
cards.summary: Match the coding editor orientation shown in the reference: dense file tree beside a real source editor with path context, gutter, dirty state, restored view position, and active-file reveal.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: editor shows breadcrumb path context, stable CodeMirror line gutter, selected file highlight, active-file reveal via React Arborist scrollTo, restored per-file selection/scroll state, dirty markers in editor and rail, and screenshot QA artifacts for selected, dirty, save-error, no-file, missing, and 1024x640 narrow states in docs/qa/editor-parity/.

cards.title: Active file sync across rail and editor
cards.summary: The user must always know which file is active. File rail selection, editor tab title, path bar, watcher refreshes, and workspace restore need to agree.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: activeFileByWorkspace persists by canonical workspace root; relaunch restores the saved file into rail selection, editor tab/path, and content; stale paths are cleared; watcher refresh reconciles the selected file to the fresh tree node when it still exists and preserves the missing-file warning when it disappears. Native screenshot proof: docs/qa/active-file-sync/restore.png.

cards.title: Per-file editor view state
cards.summary: Switching files should not throw away the user's place. Cursor, selection, scroll, and focus are part of the editor experience.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: per-file view state captures selection anchor/head, scrollTop, and focus intent. Native smoke set first.ts to Ln 5, Col 9, switched to second.ts, switched back, restored the same line/column, then ArrowDown advanced to Ln 6, Col 1, proving CodeMirror focus was restored. Proof screenshots: docs/qa/editor-view-state/restore.png and docs/qa/editor-view-state/focus-restored.png.

cards.title: Dirty draft protection
cards.summary: The userflow audit found the main trust risk: unsaved work can be lost when changing files/projects. This must be blocked before the editor feels real.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: dirty file/workspace replacement opens a Save/Discard/Cancel modal with the file name and consequence. Save continues only after write_text_file succeeds; failed saves keep the draft and pending navigation. Covered by npm test flows for save failure, save success, discard, no pending navigation, prompt trigger logic, and modal copy; screenshot proof: docs/qa/dirty-draft-protection/modal.png.

cards.title: Save failure recovery
cards.summary: Save failures need a recovery flow, not only a red message. The buffer must remain safe and the next action must be obvious.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: failed saves keep the edited buffer, show a scoped Save failed alert with the human error, expose Retry and Open externally, disable duplicate retry/save while a write is running, and clear the error only when the real write path succeeds. Covered by npm build/test and screenshot proof in docs/qa/editor-parity/save-error.png.

cards.title: Editor responsive screenshot QA
cards.summary: The userflow audit called out screenshot proof for editor states and small desktop widths before claiming parity.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: npm run qa:editor captures selected, dirty, save-error, missing-file, no-file, and 1024x640 narrow workbench states into docs/qa/editor-parity/. Visual audit confirmed clear rail/editor/terminal orientation with no overlapping chrome in narrow and no-file states.

cards.title: Editor find and replace
cards.summary: Basic file editing needs local find/replace; otherwise the editor is too thin to replace the VS Code surface Jason actually uses.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: CodeMirror search is included as a first-class editor extension with Cmd+F/F3/Cmd+G search keybindings, a visible Find button, and replace/replace-all support through the editor-native search panel. Screenshot proof: docs/qa/editor-parity/find-replace.png.

cards.title: Editor language modes
cards.summary: The first editor pass needs credible syntax support for the file types this repo and Jason's projects use most.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: editor language extensions cover TypeScript, TSX/JSX, JavaScript, Markdown, HTML, CSS, Rust, JSON, TOML, YAML, and shell scripts/dotfiles. Covered by npm build and focused mode mapping tests.

cards.title: External change and save conflict handling
cards.summary: Agents and terminals can edit files while the editor is open. The app must not silently overwrite external changes.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: read_text_file returns modifiedMs, write_text_file receives the last-seen modifiedMs, and the Rust backend rejects stale writes before overwriting. The editor conflict surface offers Reload, Overwrite, and Open externally. Covered by cargo test stale-write rejection, npm build/test, and screenshot proof at docs/qa/editor-parity/save-conflict.png.

cards.title: Large and binary file policy
cards.summary: The editor should fail safely on huge or binary files instead of freezing the app.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: backend rejects files over the 2 MB editor cap before full text load, rejects invalid UTF-8/binary files explicitly, and rejects oversized save buffers without touching disk. Covered by cargo tests for large read, binary read, and large save rejection.

cards.title: Basic file operations
cards.summary: Replacing the VS Code shell requires basic rail operations, not only open-file. These commands must be reachable from the rail, menu/shortcut paths, and the file context menu where relevant.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: create file, create folder, rename, delete, duplicate, and reveal in Finder work from the rail/context menu. Backend guards every mutation against escaping the workspace root; destructive operations confirm; dirty active files are protected. Covered by npm build/test, cargo test file-op guard/roundtrip coverage, and git diff --check.

cards.title: Open files and dirty-state tabs
cards.summary: Let the user bounce between a few files without losing place. This is the minimum editor workflow, not a full IDE tab system.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-08: multiple open files appear as tabs with close controls; switching tabs preserves in-memory drafts; dirty files are marked in tabs and the rail; dirty tab close confirms before discard; rename/delete keeps open tabs aligned. Covered by npm build/test, editorTabs helper tests, screenshot QA, and visual selected/narrow inspection.

cards.title: Agent command profiles
cards.summary: Stop hardcoding one command forever. Add simple profiles for Codex, Gemini, Claude, and shell so the pane can launch the desired CLI without burning the wrong quota during testing.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: terminal header exposes Codex, Gemini, Claude, and Shell profiles; fresh state defaults to Codex; shows active command/mode details; successful changes restart the pane through open_workspace and persist to workspace.json; missing-command errors stay visible and do not overwrite the saved profile. Covered by npm build/test, cargo missing-command preflight tests, screenshot QA, selected/narrow visual inspection, and docs/local-state.md schema update.

cards.title: Product name and README docs
cards.summary: Lock the user-facing product name and give contributors a clear README/doc map without renaming the repo/package slug prematurely.
cards.done: Product name is Keelhouse across PRD, README, product positioning, app metadata, generated roadmap, and docs index; repo/package/storage slug remains agent-cli until an explicit migration.

cards.title: Codex-style agent composer
cards.summary: Keep terminal panes as the trusted source-of-truth agent interface, but add a bottom Codex-style composer for sending prompts/instructions to the selected real agent pane or triggering app-owned actions.
cards.done: Composer can target the selected Claude/Codex/shell pane, send multiline text safely through the real pty, show target project/session/pane context, stop/interrupt the pane, retain draft/history, handle Enter/Shift+Enter/Escape predictably, and run app actions via command palette routing; it never replaces the underlying terminal transcript.

cards.title: VS Code shortcut baseline
cards.summary: Keep VS Code muscle memory for the supported workflow: open folder, quick open/search, save, close, copy/paste, terminal focus, new pane, split/switch pane, and command palette.
cards.done: A documented shortcut map covers the v0.5 workflow, matches VS Code defaults where applicable, appears in menu items/tooltips where relevant, and explicitly lists terminal/editor/app-chrome conflicts and exceptions.

cards.title: Robust terminal parity
cards.summary: The terminal is the trusted agent surface, so it needs daily-driver robustness beyond the initial working loop.
cards.done: Single-pane terminal verifies Claude/Codex TUIs, alternate screen apps, ANSI/truecolor styling, resize, scrollback, mouse selection, copy, paste, bracketed paste, common keyboard chords, command/cwd/status header, fast-output responsiveness, and screenshot/runtime QA.

cards.title: Right-click context menus
cards.summary: macOS pointer workflows need right-click/Control-click affordances, not only visible buttons. Context menus should expose the same command registry as menu bar, shortcuts, and command palette where possible.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: current surfaces expose right-click/Control-click menus for the workspace root, project/session rows, file explorer, editor tabs, editor text surface, browser preview, terminal pane, and composer/agent controls. Menus share shortcut labels where active, show disabled and danger states, reuse existing destructive confirmations/dirty guards, and are documented in docs/context-menus.md. Git-marked file rows can open read-only diffs, stage, unstage, and discard unstaged changes.

cards.title: Codex-level chrome polish system
cards.summary: Define the workbench chrome system before more surfaces drift: dense rails, calm dark surfaces, tokens, menus, badges, pane headers, settings/sidebar rhythm, command palette, toasts, and restrained motion.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: current v0.5 chrome uses primitive -> semantic -> component CSS tokens, shared control/menu/badge/field/overlay states, visible focus, disabled/danger/success/warning states, reduced-motion handling, and screenshot QA for one-project workbench, context menu, recovery alerts, narrow layout, and a chrome state palette. Icon replacement remains ICON-SYSTEM; browser and diff/review now consume these tokens, while settings and command palette remain future surfaces.

cards.title: Codex-quality icon and status system
cards.summary: The app should feel like a polished Codex-style workbench, not a raw prototype. Define one consistent icon language for project rail, file explorer, editor, browser, terminal, agent panes, git state, and common actions.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: current v0.5 chrome uses lucide-react through app/src/icons.tsx as one SVG icon contract. File-tree object icons, rail actions, editor actions, terminal status pills, composer controls, browser preview, source-control/diff entry points, context-menu rows, and QA fixtures use currentColor icons on a 16px dense grid. Non-universal controls keep labels, tooltips, or accessible names. Pane status and future activity status mappings cover thinking/running/waiting/error/exited/complete. Source-host and multi-pane-specific icons remain deferred until those real surfaces land.

cards.title: Keyboard and focus accessibility baseline
cards.summary: A dense tool still needs visible focus, keyboard reachability, and labelled controls so it can be used without pointer precision.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: current v0.5 chrome has explicit labels for rail actions, workspace root, agent profile picker, terminal canvas, composer draft, browser preview controls, and diff review controls; editor tabs put tab semantics on the actual focusable buttons; the terminal canvas is tabbable and has a visible focus ring; context menus focus the first enabled item, support Arrow Up/Down, and close with Escape. Full modal trapping, canvas transcript screen-reader review, and deeper settings/multi-pane accessibility rules remain deferred.

cards.title: Left project rail for open workspaces
cards.summary: The core multi-project workflow: Project A, Project B, Project C live in one app instead of separate VS Code windows. Use a persistent left project/workspace rail, similar in spirit to Codex's sidebar, rather than relying only on top tabs.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: the left rail now has persisted openProjects separate from recents, shows at least three project rows in QA, marks active/running/exited/attention states, promotes opened folders into the rail, prunes missing paths, and exposes switch/reveal/copy/close context-menu actions. Switching projects uses the real workspace load path and restores in-session editor tabs, active file, editor buffers, and view state. Project session rows, browser preview URL state, and multiple live panes are now implemented; richer lifecycle state and cross-relaunch session restore stay with their dedicated later cards.

cards.title: Project session rows
cards.summary: Translate Codex chat rows into named task/workbench sessions under each project. These are not editor tabs and not custom chat threads; they are saved work contexts for agent work.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: projectSessions and activeSessionByProject persist in workspace.json; the rail shows nested session rows with active/running/exited/attention states; New session, switch, rename, copy name, delete with last-session protection, and Show more/Show fewer are implemented; selecting sessions snapshots/restores editor tabs, active file, buffers, and view state for current in-memory sessions. Browser URLs and live pane state are implemented; pane labels, transcript references, layout restore, and cross-relaunch snapshot restore remain with their dedicated later cards.

cards.title: Browser and web preview pane
cards.summary: The stripped VS Code workbench still needs a browser surface for localhost apps, docs, auth flows, and agent-generated pages without context switching.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: the workbench has a docked iframe browser preview pane between editor and terminal with Back, Forward, Reload, address/local URL input, Open, and Open externally controls. Preview URLs are normalized, remembered per project and active project session in workspace.json, restored on project/session switch, and exposed through a browser context menu with copy/open/navigation actions. Dev-server auto-detection and agent-controlled browser actions remain with their dedicated later cards.

cards.title: Multiple agent panes per project
cards.summary: Jason often runs multiple Claude/Codex sessions against the same project, and different open projects may run different agents at the same time. Add a pane manager that can create/switch/close multiple terminal panes per project.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: backend PtyState owns multiple real pty/process panes keyed by pane id; open_workspace spawns/focuses a pane without killing panes from other projects; create_pane, focus_pane, and close_pane add, focus, and safely kill exactly one selected pane. Grid events include paneId; the frontend caches snapshots by pane and paints only the focused pane. The terminal header exposes a focused pane strip plus New pane profile, New, and Close controls. Project/session state aggregates all panes for the project. Pane names, restart/kill-all, layout restore, and app-owned handles remain with their dedicated later cards.

cards.title: Pane names and task labels
cards.summary: When several agents run in one project, each pane needs a human label so Jason can remember what it is doing.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: terminal panes have editable task labels shown in the pane strip and composer target. Labels normalize input, fall back to profile/index names when blank, persist in workspace.json by project-session plus pane slot, and can be edited by double-clicking the pane pill or using Rename Selected Pane in the terminal context menu.

cards.title: App-owned agent session handles
cards.summary: Define the missing middle layer between real pty panes and the Codex-style composer: a stable app-owned handle for each pane/session.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: app/src/agentSessionHandle.ts defines stable pane-backed handles with project/session ids, cwd, pane label, profile id/label, process state, approval mode, exit code, created time, activity metadata, and send/interrupt/readTail/close operations. The composer now sends prompts through the active handle; Stop and Close route through handle methods; terminal context menu can copy the latest 20 rendered terminal lines through readTail. Activity log and future hooks can target the handle contract instead of UI component state.

cards.title: Agent activity and thinking states
cards.summary: Background agents need Codex-style visible status and activity rows when they think, run commands, edit files, call tools, wait for approval/input, error, exit, or complete. Show concise activity/progress summaries and tool events, not hidden chain-of-thought.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: app/src/agentActivity.ts normalizes in-memory activity rows scoped by project/session/pane. The selected pane shows a current status row from its handle plus recent rows for prompt sent, app command ran/failed, edited file, created pane, stop sent, copied output, pane closed, complete, and command failed on non-zero exit. Rows render above the composer with shared icons/status colors and accessible labels; the QA fixture covers running, prompt, edited file, ran command, and failed command rows. Approval rows and durable/filterable history are covered by APP-ACTIONS-MINIMAL and AGENT-ACTIVITY-LOG.

cards.title: Agent activity timeline
cards.summary: Each pane/session needs an inspectable timeline of user-safe agent events so Jason can see what happened without reading every terminal line.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: agentActivityEvents now persists up to 200 user-safe events with project/session/pane attribution, kind, label, detail, status, timestamp, target, exit code, output reference, and undo hint fields. The selected pane shows a durable timeline below the activity strip with filters for prompts, process, commands, files, tools, git, app actions, approvals, browser, errors, and completion. Startup normalization drops malformed stored rows. Current producers cover prompts, app commands, app-action approvals including Open Diff, file saves, pane lifecycle actions, copied output, and process exits; git mutation, richer browser, and tool producers populate the reserved event fields when those real surfaces land.

cards.title: Minimal app-owned action gate
cards.summary: Permission controls need real enforcement behind them. Add the small action gate for app-owned actions before showing Codex-style approval modes.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: app/src/appActions.ts defines app-owned action descriptors with kind, target, risk class, requester, reason, and undo hint; resolves each action to an audit event with approval mode, decision, prompt state, timestamp, and reason. Existing app-owned paths for focus pane, open file, open diff, open browser preview, attach reference, interrupt process, create pane, close pane, and composer commands route through the gate. Prompted, denied, blocked, and composer-originated decisions log approval audit rows to the activity timeline.

cards.title: Composer harness controls
cards.summary: Grow the bottom composer into a Codex-style app-owned harness around real terminal panes: permission mode, goal state, target pane, model/profile, attachments, and stop/send controls.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: composer state is scoped by project/session in composerHarnessBySession; the bottom harness shows target project/session/pane, permission mode, profile selector, goal, attachment controls, and send/stop state. Ask, Approve safe, and Full access drive the app-owned action gate. Local file, current editor file, and browser preview/screenshot references can be attached and removed. Prompt sends transparently include goal/attachment context when present, and permission, goal, profile, attachment, prompt, and app-action events log to the activity timeline.

cards.title: Agent process lifecycle controls
cards.summary: A cockpit needs basic process affordances: restart, kill, status, exited state, and command/cwd visibility.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: backend terminate_pane kills a pane child process while keeping the pane/transcript for exit state; backend restart_pane spawns and focuses a fresh process, then removes/kills the replaced process. The frontend preserves pane slot and label across restart, updates project/session status, clears stale snapshots, logs terminate/restart events, and routes both actions through the action gate. The terminal header shows command, mode, cwd, state, Restart, Kill, New, and Close, with narrow responsive icon controls.

cards.title: Agent-first resizable tray workbench
cards.summary: Correct the cockpit hierarchy: readable terminal-backed Run output and the composer own the main screen, while raw terminal, editor, and browser are explicit switchable/resizable surfaces.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: Run presents the visible active terminal viewport without inventing provider chat or hidden reasoning; app-owned activity remains separate provenance below it. Raw terminal switches to the real canvas. First open hides editor/browser tools; one compact Tools menu opens Editor, Browser, or Split and docks them left/right/bottom. Verified in the actual React shell at 1440/1024/900 and native Tauri with live PTY output.

cards.title: Keelhouse shell chrome
cards.summary: Correct the outer application chrome toward the useful VS Code/Codex structure without copying controls that do not fit Keelhouse.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: the app shell now uses titlebar/sidebar/main/status grid geometry. The header shows real Keelhouse state and actions: workspace, session, selected agent/pane, process status, Open Folder, and Reload Preview. Fake traffic lights, fake back/forward, the search-shaped folder picker, and the nonfunctional activity rail were removed. Activity drawer navigation remains parked until it switches real drawer content.

cards.title: Projects and Files drawer modes
cards.summary: Split the side drawer into real modes instead of stacking project/session rows above the file tree.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: the side drawer has Projects and Files tabs. Projects shows open projects and task sessions; Files shows workspace root, file actions, and the virtualized tree. The Files drawer reattaches its height observer when opened. Screenshot QA defaults to Projects mode. Verified with npm run build, npm test, npm run qa:editor, selected screenshot inspection, and git diff --check.

cards.title: Switchable activity drawers
cards.summary: Continue drawer navigation only when it is functional, not decorative. Borrow Zed's agentic layout principle: project/session thread sidebar plus rearrangeable real panels, not a decorative VS Code rail clone. Remaining useful drawers are Search, Source Control/Git, Browser/tools, and Settings.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: the side drawer has Projects, Files, Search, Git, Browser, and Settings modes. Search filters the live file tree and opens editor matches; Git reads real git status through Rust; Browser controls the preview URL/history/reload/external open actions; Settings changes current profile, permission mode, agent surface, tray position, open folder, and file refresh. Drawer width is pointer/keyboard resizable; collapsed icon-only state and width persist locally. Verified with npm run build, npm test, cargo test, npm run qa:editor, selected screenshot inspection, and git diff --check.

cards.title: Restore projects, files, panes
cards.summary: After quit/relaunch, the cockpit should come back as the same working context, without pretending to preserve live agent process state.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: sessionEditorSnapshots persists per-session editor tabs, active file, dirty buffers, and CodeMirror view state. paneLayoutsBySession persists pane slots, launch profile ids, and labels. Startup normalizes both stores before reopening the last workspace and creates fresh panes from the saved layout without pretending live process memory or transcripts survived relaunch. Covered by npm run build and npm test -- sessionRestore.test.ts.

cards.title: Git status in file rail
cards.summary: Small but high-signal replacement for a VS Code affordance Jason likely relies on: dirty/new/deleted markers in the file tree.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: file nodes show compact Git tokens for modified, untracked/new, added, deleted, renamed, and staged files. Deleted files that no longer exist on disk are inserted as virtual file rows under their parent folder and cannot be opened as real files. Unsaved editor draft dots remain separate from repository status. Covered by npm run build, npm test -- fileGitStatus.test.ts, and npm run qa:editor.

cards.title: Diff view for agent changes
cards.summary: Jason supervises agents by reading what changed. A file status marker is not enough; he needs to inspect diffs without opening VS Code.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: Source Control rows and file-rail Git context menus open a read-only unified diff review in the editor tray. The review shows source/status, additions/deletions, old/new line numbers, diff metadata, synthetic untracked text diffs, and hunk-to-editor jumps for files that still exist. Covered by npm run build, npm test -- diffView.test.ts, cargo test git_, and cargo test synthesizes_untracked_text_diff.

cards.title: Lightweight git review actions
cards.summary: Add only the git actions needed to review/clean agent work, not a full git client.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: diff review and Git-marked file context menus expose Stage, Unstage, Discard Unstaged Changes, and Copy shown diff. The blind audit findings were folded into the card: discard is blocked when the same file has an unsaved editor draft; mixed staged/unstaged files use explicit labels (Copy shown diff, whole-file Stage/Unstage); backend git_file_action validates repository-relative paths and re-reads target status before mutation; staged new files, tracked discard, and untracked discard use separate Git command paths; real temp-repo Git tests cover stage/unstage/discard; the slice stops at per-file actions, not branch/commit/push/PR; screenshot QA includes diff-review.png. Covered by npm run build, npm test, cargo test git_file_actions, npm run qa:editor, and git diff --check.

cards.title: Accepted chrome demo contract
cards.summary: Treat demo/keelhouse-chrome-demo.html as the visual contract for the app pass.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: the accepted demo contract is documented in docs/chrome-contract.md, enforced by npm run qa:chrome-contract, and tied to accepted demo plus real app shell checks.

cards.title: Chrome screenshot QA gate
cards.summary: Every chrome pass must prove the accepted states still hold visually and mechanically.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: npm run qa:shell captures the actual React shell at 1440x900, 1024x640, and 900x640; npm run qa:chrome-contract requires those artifacts plus the accepted token/control/run-surface contract. The deterministic editor fixture remains for menu, diff, modal, and failure states but is no longer accepted as sole app-shell proof. Native Tauri proof is stored at docs/qa/app-shell/native-run.png.

cards.title: Graphite and steel-cyan theme gate
cards.summary: Lock the accepted graphite shell and steel-cyan accent into semantic tokens and QA checks.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-09: app chrome defines steel-cyan primitive tokens, routes semantic accent tokens through them, removes the old blue primary treatment, flattens shared badge/control radii, and the chrome contract gate rejects warm accent drift.

cards.title: Terminal-backed agent run surface
cards.summary: Readable live agent output is the working surface; raw terminal is available for direct TUI interaction and activity remains separate provenance.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: Run is the default surface and renders the visible active terminal viewport without inventing provider-native chat or hidden reasoning. Observable app-owned activity is filtered separately below it, the composer stays pinned at the bottom, and Raw terminal switches to the real canvas for direct TUI interaction. Structured provider messages remain DIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS or explicit hook/adapter work.

cards.title: Port accepted chrome into app
cards.summary: Port the accepted demo into the real Tauri app shell without losing current v1 functionality.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: the real Tauri shell opens at 1440x900 with a direct project/session drawer, terminal-backed Run output, observable activity, compact composer, and hidden first-open tool tray. Existing project, editor, browser, Git, pane, composer, lifecycle, command-palette, and search code paths remain covered by the full frontend/Rust suites and deterministic state fixtures. Actual-shell screenshots cover 1440/1024/900 and docs/qa/app-shell/native-run.png proves live PTY output in Tauri.

cards.title: Native-feeling controls and icons
cards.summary: Flatten obvious AI-looking rounded rectangles into native-feeling toolbar, menu, dropdown, and command controls.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: redundant recent-activity chips and nine inline tray/dock buttons were removed. Drawer modes are icon tabs; run settings and tool/dock choices use compact labelled popovers; toolbar controls stay flat with shared outline icons, focus/hover/disabled states, and labels only for ambiguous commands. Actual-shell and native screenshots confirm the reduced chrome.

cards.title: Movable resizable tool trays
cards.summary: Editor and browser are optional resizable tools around the agent run, not a permanent stacked workbench.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: first open hides the tool tray, opening Tools defaults to Editor, and one compact menu exposes Editor, Browser, Split, left, right, bottom, and hidden states. Layout and sizes persist; narrow side docks become bottom docks; hidden-tray CSS is regression-tested at 900px; single-tool modes remove the unused surface and splitter.

cards.title: Workbench chrome coherence pass
cards.summary: Fix the actual shell before measurement using VS Code/Zed-level chrome craft without copying either product, with demo/cockpit-demo.html as the graphite mood reference and Codex as the clean-composition reference: one large agent-first window, a direct thread/project sidebar on the left, no decorative left activity rail, flat toolbar icons, steel-cyan accent, minimal rounded button chrome, resizable splitters, and editor/browser/git/raw terminal as docked tool panes only when needed. Target walkthrough: demo/keelhouse-chrome-demo.html.
cards.done: Verified 2026-07-10: the actual first-open shell reads as one agent-first cockpit at 1440, 1024, and the 900px minimum. Projects/sessions remain a direct left drawer; Run owns the center; editor/browser are hidden until requested; compact menus replace button clusters; splitters and responsive geometry are typed and tested. Deterministic fixtures retain menu/settings/palette/error coverage, while actual React and native Tauri screenshots prove the shell and live PTY path.

cards.title: Local dev server detection
cards.summary: When Claude/Codex starts Vite, Next, Astro, or another dev server, the app should make the local preview obvious without acting like a full browser. Current slice detects local URLs from terminal output and offers an explicit browser-preview action.
cards.done: Detected localhost URLs from terminal output can be opened in the browser preview; the preview remembers the latest URL per project/session and offers reload/open-external. OS-level port discovery remains deferred until daily-driver metrics show it is needed. Executed 2026-07-11: detection, gated open, per-project/session preview persistence, and reload/open-external all shipped and test-covered (browserPreview.test.ts); OS-level port discovery stays deferred per criteria.

cards.title: VS Code-style command palette
cards.summary: A compact VS Code-style way to expose actions without adding IDE chrome. Current slice adds a real Shift+Cmd+P palette for existing app-owned actions: open folder, save/find/close tab, pane create/restart/kill/close/clear, browser reload/open detected, drawer switches, tray switches, and composer context attachments.
cards.done: A keyboard palette can run core app actions, shows shortcuts where assigned, and stays consistent with menu/context-menu labels. Current slice keeps Cmd+P quick-open, ripgrep text search, and terminal scrollback search with SEARCH and TERMINAL-FIND. Executed 2026-07-11: palette runs core app actions with shortcut labels consistent with menus/context menus; demo overlay parity applied; quick-open/text-search live under SEARCH.

cards.title: Find files and text
cards.summary: A lightweight replacement for the VS Code search/file-jump behaviors that matter while supervising agents. Current slice adds Cmd+P Quick Open and Files/Text scopes in the Search drawer.
cards.done: Keyboard-driven file quick-open and ripgrep-backed text search work inside the selected project. Current text search uses rg --json when available, falls back to the ignore-aware walker, caps file size/results, and opens hits at the matching line. Executed 2026-07-11: Cmd+P quick-open and rg-backed text search verified (workspaceSearch.test.ts, cargo workspace_text_search). Cross-project/session search deferred: no observed daily-driver need yet (anti-drift), revisit after DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE.

cards.title: Daily-driver success metrics
cards.summary: Readiness gate for the three target replacement workflows (one-project edit+agent+preview, two-agent same-project, three-project switch/relaunch): verifies the required surfaces, docs, and screenshot evidence exist and writes docs/qa/daily-driver reports. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 clarification: this card proves readiness only — timed live runs, VS Code comparison, and the North-Star claim belong to DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE and must not be cited as proven from this gate.
cards.done: Script and record three workflow runs: one-project edit+agent, two-agent same-project, and three-project switch/relaunch. Current readiness gate verifies required app surfaces, docs, and screenshots exist and writes docs/qa/daily-driver/latest.json plus latest.md; timed live Tauri runs, elapsed time, crashes, memory/CPU snapshots, and VS Code-needed checks remain next. Executed 2026-07-11 as the readiness gate (qa:daily-driver green, evidence at docs/qa/daily-driver/); timed live runs and VS Code comparison are DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE scope, not this card.

cards.title: Resource and jank budget
cards.summary: Prove this is lighter than the VS Code workflow it replaces across 1-project, 2-agent, and 3-project task runs. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 addition: a concrete render-perf gate before v1 — measure IPC payload size at fast-output rates, Canvas 2D frame time at 1, 2, and 4 concurrent panes, and a jank threshold (frame-drop rate), so the deferred dirty-region/WebGL decisions trigger from data instead of surprise.
cards.done: Static budgets green plus recorded render-perf numbers (IPC payload, frame times at 1/2/4 panes, jank rate) with thresholds; packaged Keelhouse vs VS Code memory/CPU comparison recorded with DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE.

cards.title: Find in terminal scrollback
cards.summary: Agent output can be long. Search inside terminal scrollback is necessary for reviewing logs and prior agent output.
cards.done: Find text in the active pane scrollback, jump next/previous, and preserve terminal focus behavior. Executed 2026-07-11: backend Screen-space search + viewport jump with real-VT Rust test, find strip UI with prev/next/count/preview, palette command; docs/terminal-robustness.md records scope and deferred regex/cell-highlighting.

cards.title: Codex-style settings parity
cards.summary: Borrow Codex's searchable left-nav settings shape and icon density, but keep only categories that serve the lean VS Code replacement workflow.
cards.done: Settings has searchable grouped sections for General, Appearance, App configuration, Behavior, Keyboard shortcuts, MCP servers, Browser preview, Agent hooks, Connections, Git, Environments, and Worktrees; Profile, Pets, Usage & billing, Archived chats, and Chat Settings are absent; Appshots and Computer use remain parked. Executed 2026-07-11: searchable settings modal per the docs/settings-parity.md chrome spec with only real categories (General agent/permission, Layout dock/tray/reset, Browser preview URL, Git health); opens via drawer Settings button, settings.open palette command, and Cmd+, (chrome.settings shortcut registered); dropped/deferred categories absent; helpers+component tested (132 vitest); visual proof captured in dev shell.

cards.title: Settings for commands, themes, and shortcuts
cards.summary: Keep settings boring and inspectable: agent commands, AI connections, source-control connections, ignored folders, font, color theme, default layout, browser preview, hooks, git/worktree defaults, environments, and shortcut overrides. This is not a plugin platform.
cards.done: A local config file controls agent profiles, AI connections, source-control connections, font/theme, ignored folders, default pane layout, browser preview, hooks, Git/worktree behavior, environments, and app-level keybindings; bad config reports actionable errors and plugins/extensions are not supported. Executed 2026-07-11: settings modal now covers agent commands (launch profiles), permission default, layout, browser preview URL, git health, ignored-folders (read-only mirror of the backend filter), theme/font status, and a read-only active-shortcut reference table from shortcuts.ts. AI connections stay with AI-CONNECTIONS; shortcut overrides stay with KEYBINDINGS-CONFIG; theme variants stay with THEME.

cards.title: Configurable keybindings
cards.summary: After the VS Code-compatible default shortcut map stabilizes, allow focused overrides without hardcoding personal preferences into the app.
cards.done: A local config file can override app-level shortcuts, reports conflicts with terminal/editor reserved bindings, and generates a visible shortcut reference. Executed 2026-07-11: keybindingOverrides persist in workspace.json (normalized on load, active shortcuts only), chrome-scope key handling resolves through comboMatches/resolveShortcutKeys so overrides apply live and labels update everywhere via shortcutKeys, conflict detection warns inline in the settings Keyboard shortcuts category, per-row Rebind capture (single combo, Escape cancels) + Reset for active shortcuts; native-menu accelerators and CodeMirror keymaps stay registry-bound (documented boundary). 136 vitest incl. override/conflict/combo tests.

cards.title: Color themes
cards.summary: Support practical color themes across chrome, terminal, rail, and editor. Start with the approved mono-ghost theme; do not expand into plugin theming or break the chrome token contract.
cards.done: App chrome, terminal canvas, file rail, and editor can switch between built-in color themes with readable contrast while preserving semantic token roles and component states. Executed 2026-07-11: mono-ghost theme as a token-only [data-theme] remap (component contract preserved; recurring hexes promoted to tokens first), persisted appTheme setting with selector in Settings, contract gate green with graphite as reference, mono-ghost verified in the running shell.

cards.title: AI connections and credentials settings
cards.summary: Users need to hook up real AI tooling: Claude/Codex CLI auth, provider API keys, model defaults, MCP servers, environment variables, permission defaults, and health checks.
cards.done: Settings can configure Claude/Codex command profiles, provider/API credentials via OS-safe storage, MCP server entries, env vars, default models, permission policy, and validation checks that explain missing auth or broken connections.

cards.title: Built-in agent MCP/API hooks
cards.summary: Give Claude/Codex-style agents a first-class way to cooperate with the app without making a plugin platform: inspect projects/panes/open files, request focus/split/new-pane actions, open diffs, and report task status through app-owned commands.
cards.done: A local permissioned MCP/API endpoint exposes documented app-owned commands; actions that mutate files, git state, panes, or project layout require visible approval/undo where appropriate; every agent action is attributed in the UI/log.

cards.title: Pane transcript capture
cards.summary: Completed agent sessions should be reviewable after the process exits without keeping panes open forever.
cards.done: A pane can save a plain-text transcript/log path for later review, with clear retention/reset behavior. Executed 2026-07-12: pane transcripts captured on exit and via a Save Transcript context action, persisted per project/session in paneTranscripts (capped 60 / 100k chars, empty dropped, tail-truncated), reviewable in a read-only modal (Review Transcripts palette command) listing by pane label + relative time. Pure helpers tested (145 vitest). Text-only, no ANSI replay.

cards.title: Background agent notifications
cards.summary: When an agent in another project or pane stops or needs input, Jason should not have to manually inspect every pane.
cards.done: Background panes can surface in-app badges and optional macOS notifications for exit/attention events. Executed 2026-07-12: background-exit badge count on project-rail rows (backgroundExits.ts pure helpers + tests), cleared on project focus; optional native macOS notification via tauri-plugin-notification gated behind a default-off Settings toggle that only requests permission when enabled. 141 vitest / 36 cargo.

cards.title: Worktree helper
cards.summary: One keystroke: new pane = git worktree add + agent launched inside it, named and disposable. The 'multiple agents on one repo, parallel' workflow.
cards.done: New Worktree Pane / Remove Worktree wired end-to-end: Rust create_project_worktree/remove_project_worktree (git worktree add/remove --force + branch -D), App.tsx createWorktreePane/closeWorktreePane, worktrees.ts store helpers persisted to workspace.json. Verified: cargo test 39/39 (incl. a real-repo worktree add/remove round-trip test), npm test 152/152 (incl. worktrees.test.ts), npm run build, cargo fmt --check, qa:chrome-contract all green. Gap: no live `npm run tauri dev` smoke test creating/removing a real worktree pane through the UI yet — build-verified, not native-UI-verified.

cards.title: Integration adapter policy
cards.summary: Define the allowed lane for other integrations so Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Linear, Jira, Slack, or Discord do not turn the app into a plugin hub.
cards.done: Documented integration policy classifies integrations as core, first-class, adapter-lane, parked, or out-of-scope; every integration requires a concrete workflow, health check, credential boundary, and app-owned command surface. Executed 2026-07-11: policy section added to docs/integrations-scope.md with the five lanes, four per-integration requirements, parked/out-of-scope lists, and promotion paths.

cards.title: GitHub and GitLab connections
cards.summary: Local Git is not enough once agent work needs to move through PR/MR review. Add first-class source-hosting connections without becoming a full forge client.
cards.done: Settings detects `git`, `gh`, and `glab` auth health; supports GitHub, GitLab.com, and self-hosted GitLab remotes; opens repo/PR/MR/issue/pipeline links in browser preview or external browser; shows source-host status for the active repo; credentials are stored only through OS-safe storage.

cards.title: Local app packaging
cards.summary: Make it launch like a real macOS app, not only from `npm run tauri dev`.
cards.done: A local signed or ad-hoc packaged .app launches, opens folders, and runs agents with documented setup requirements.

cards.title: Session archive
cards.summary: Only after project sessions and transcripts exist, add a way to hide old sessions without turning the product into archived chats.
cards.done: A completed project session can be archived/restored; archived sessions retain transcript references and metadata, stay out of the main rail, and are labeled as work sessions rather than chats. Executed 2026-07-12: optional archived flag on ProjectSession (normalized on load, backward-compatible), Archive/Unarchive session context action with last-active-session protection, rail hides archived by default with a Show archived (n) toggle; pure helpers (setProjectSessionArchived/activeSessionsForRail/archivedSessionCount) tested (146 vitest).

cards.title: Optional direct API agent harness
cards.summary: Research-backed future lane: app-owned direct API/MCP agent workflows using human review, resumable state, and tracing, without replacing real Claude/Codex terminal panes.
cards.done: A feature-flagged direct agent can run app-owned tools/MCP with approval gates, state restore/resume, tracing/activity log entries, and clear separation from terminal-backed Claude/Codex panes.

cards.title: Mine old projects for reusable pieces
cards.summary: Re-check Hallmark/hashmark/brutal/indx for reusable Tauri shell, persistence, editor, or design patterns without adopting their product shape.
cards.done: Reusable components/patterns are listed with exact source paths and either imported, adapted, or explicitly rejected. Executed 2026-07-12: docs/reuse-audit.md surveys spike-ghostty-vt (shipped as the terminal foundation), spike editor test (decision input), rockmap (live roadmap tooling), both demos (chrome + mono-ghost), and ghostty config as pulled-in; zellij trial, superconductor reference, and rockmap schema as parked. No dead reuse debt; one licensing watch-item (superconductor super.icns must not ship — flagged for PACKAGING).

cards.title: Demo control grammar: flat actions
cards.summary: Replace the boxed default-button chrome with the accepted demo's three-control grammar: flat text actions (weight 600, hover to accent-strong), transparent icon buttons (color-only hover), and a single filled Send. Danger actions (Kill, Stop, Discard) become flat text-actions in danger color; confirmations keep existing modals. Hit areas stay at least 40px via padding even where glyphs are 16px.
cards.done: No default-variant control in titlebar, terminal toolbar, editor actions, browser toolbar, composer, or drawer renders with the boxed control background and border. Only Send (filled accent) and modal-confirm primaries are filled. 1440/1024/900 qa:shell screenshots show flat chrome; focus rings and 40px hit areas verified; npm test and npm run build pass. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

cards.title: Run surface demo composition
cards.summary: Compose the center as one continuous agent thread: terminal-backed transcript and app-owned provenance events flow inline, the composer stays anchored at the bottom, and raw terminal access lives in a dedicated bottom tray. Do not infer provider chat, tools, or hidden reasoning from terminal text.
cards.done: Corrected 2026-07-12 after native comparison with the approved demo: removed the separate Run card and Activity dashboard, flattened transcript and provenance into one thread surface, anchored the compact composer, and moved raw terminal access to the bottom tray. Native 1440/1024/900 captures verify the composition; provider-native structure remains adapter-owned.

cards.title: Elevated demo composer card
cards.summary: Rebuild the composer as the demo's elevated card: 12px radius, 1px #343642 border, deep soft shadow, gradient fade wrap, borderless textarea with 78px minimum, 34px bottom bar with flat chips for permission mode, goal, profile, and attachments, and a 24px filled up-arrow Send; Stop becomes a flat danger text-action.
cards.done: Composer matches the demo spec in qa:shell screenshots; Enter/Shift+Enter/Escape/Up/Down, history, app-commands, and attachment behavior regressions all pass in npm test; the 900px narrow layout keeps chips from wrapping into overlap. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

cards.title: Thread sidebar demo rhythm
cards.summary: Apply the demo thread-sidebar spec: 11px/600 uppercase section labels in faint ink, 34px minimum rows, project rows at 600 weight with 7px status dots, session rows indented 28px with real recency stamps (now/4h/1d from session last-activity), left-stripe actives, and quiet hover. Row-level pointer activation replaces boxed Switch affordances; context menus keep the full action set.
cards.done: Projects drawer matches the demo sidebar spec in screenshots; recency stamps render real relative times; switch/rename/close flows still pass their tests. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

cards.title: Titlebar and statusbar parity
cards.summary: 36px titlebar with the demo gradient, centered workspace crumb (strong project name plus faint branch from real git status), flat right action cluster with an accent-strong agent chip; 24px statusbar with three 12px sections: branch/sync/errors, agent status/panes, and shortcut hints.
cards.done: Titlebar and statusbar match the spec at all three QA widths; all existing titlebar actions remain reachable as flat labelled controls with 40px hit areas; the drag region still works in the native window. Executed 2026-07-11: native live-pty proof at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png (real Tauri window, live shell PTY in the centered Run card, populated activity, real recency stamps, persisted layout honored).

cards.title: Tabbed tray chrome, movable docks
cards.summary: Keep useWorkbenchLayout docking (left/right/bottom, resizable, persisted) while the dock renders the approved Files, Editor, Browser, and Git tab strip with steel-cyan underline actives. Files and Git are real tray surfaces, not duplicate left-drawer modes; the left column remains project threads.
cards.done: Corrected 2026-07-12: Files/Editor/Browser/Git are functional dock tabs and persistent titlebar icons. Clicking the active icon closes its panel; clicking it again restores it. Files is the first-open default with filtering/actions, and Git exposes branch/change review. At narrow widths chat remains the default; an explicitly opened tool replaces the main view instead of squeezing it.

cards.title: First open shows demo layout
cards.summary: First-open default becomes the demo layout: threads drawer open, centered run and composer, right dock open on Files, bottom tray strip visible, statusbar. Changes the default workbench layout from hidden; returning users keep their persisted layout. Supersedes the 2026-07-10 hidden-first-open correction with a DECISIONS.md entry. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 additions: if the default agent launch fails on first open, the run card shows an inline recovery banner (error, Open Folder/Settings pointers, shell-profile fallback); a discoverable Reset-layout-to-demo action exists for returning users.
cards.done: Corrected and reverified 2026-07-12: versioned layout preferences open with the approved project-thread sidebar, continuous agent thread and composer, Files-first right dock, terminal bottom tray, and statusbar. The macOS titlebar uses native overlay chrome, and Reset interface restores this default after panels or the sidebar are closed. Narrow windows default to chat while the persistent icons can reopen one full-width tool view.

cards.title: Menus and palette demo parity
cards.summary: Align existing overlays to the demo spec. Context menus: 6px radius, 252px minimum width, 24px uppercase mono section labels, 28px icon/label/shortcut rows, quiet hover. Command palette: 640px, 10px radius, 42px input, 32px rows with left-stripe active. Overlay scrim with blur. The settings modal spec (900x560, 230px left-stripe nav, 48px setting rows) is recorded in docs but ships with SETTINGS.
cards.done: Menus and palette match the spec in screenshots; keyboard nav, Escape, and focus behavior regressions pass; the settings modal spec is documented in docs/settings-parity.md for the v2 card. Executed 2026-07-11: menus/palette aligned and screenshot-verified, keyboard regressions green, settings modal spec recorded in docs/settings-parity.md.

cards.title: Chrome contract gate v2
cards.summary: Extend scripts/check-chrome-contract.mjs so boxed-button drift cannot recur silently: forbid the boxed control background on toolbar/composer/editor/browser button classes, require the text-action grammar classes, assert composer card values, run-surface centered column, sidebar section-label treatment, and tray tab-strip actives, and require docs/qa/chrome-delta side-by-side artifacts (demo reference captures plus matching app captures at 1440/1024/900). Blind-audit 2026-07-11 additions: refresh the stale pre-convergence editor-QA fixture to current chrome; titlebar crumb overflow rules (project truncates before branch, branch ellipsis over ~30 chars, detached HEAD shows short SHA); overlay side-by-side captures for context menu and palette; assert the shipped font-stack decision from CHROME-FONT-LOCK. Executes after CHROME-EYEBALL-SIGNOFF so the gate locks a human-approved state.
cards.done: The gate fails on reintroduction of boxed toolbar buttons (verified by a temporary revert); demo-reference PNGs are checked in and asserted; the editor fixture renders current chrome; crumb overflow rules implemented and tested with long branch names and detached HEAD; npm run qa:chrome-contract is green on the converged chrome.

cards.title: Structured run cards via hooks
cards.summary: The demo's structured thinking, working-plan, edited-file-with-Review, and approval cards, fed by real agent events through AGENT-HOOKS adapters and never inferred from terminal text. Reuses the card components shipped in RUN-SURFACE-COMPOSITION. Blind-audit 2026-07-11 addition: the boundary gets a mechanical provenance gate — an automated check that every rendered run card traces to an explicit event source (appActions/agentActivityEvents/hook payloads), so a future regex-on-terminal-text regression fails loudly; PRD carries the v1/v2 expectation caveat so the demo's structured cards are not read as a v1 promise.
cards.done: With a hook-connected agent, real events render as demo-spec cards; without hooks, Run remains terminal-backed with no fabricated structure; a provenance test fails if any card renders from terminal-text parsing.

cards.title: Populated-state visual proof
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: every chrome screenshot proves the empty first-open state only. Capture the surfaces that carry the product: live PTY agent output flowing through the centered run card (including the native tauri-dev smoke at docs/qa/chrome-delta/native-run.png), five-plus stacked activity rows with long real labels, a populated three-project sidebar with sessions and recency stamps, and a multi-pane strip, at 1440/1024/900. Enforce a legibility floor: the run column may never compress below 600px or scroll horizontally with drawer plus docked tray open.
cards.done: Populated screenshots checked into docs/qa/chrome-delta/ for run-with-live-output, stacked activity, three-project sidebar, and multi-pane states at all three widths; native live-pty capture exists; a test asserts the run column legibility floor; visual inspection confirms the demo composition survives real terminal density. Executed 2026-07-12: populated three-project sidebar (real session recency, real project switching, crash-recovery banner) captured at 1440/1024/900 in docs/qa/chrome-delta/density-populated-*.png; fluid run column confirmed legible at 900px with no compression. Gap: the stacked-activity-row density check was not captured — synthetic activity events for a pane the backend never spawned did not render (correctly; the app only surfaces real event provenance), and fabricating that scenario would mean mock evidence, not real proof. Real activity-density proof needs a live agent run, which belongs with DAILY-DRIVER-LIVE.

cards.title: Timed live daily-driver runs
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: DAILY-DRIVER-METRICS is a readiness gate, not proof — the North Star (replace VS Code for three workflows) has never been measured live. Run the three scenarios end-to-end in the packaged/dev Tauri app with no fixtures: one-project edit+agent+preview, two-agent same-project, three-project switch/relaunch. Time pane switch, prompt send latency, and project switch; record memory/CPU baselines and compare against the equivalent VS Code session. Include one real Gemini TUI smoke since the gemini profile has never been render-verified. Define fail thresholds before running.
cards.done: docs/qa/daily-driver/ contains timed live-run records for all three workflows with pane-switch/send/switch latencies, memory/CPU vs VS Code comparison, and a Gemini TUI render check; thresholds documented and met or failures filed as cards; STATE.md wording separates readiness evidence from live proof.

cards.title: Chrome a11y audit: contrast, targets, motion
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: the 2026-07-11 token retargets (#252732 row-active, #22242d hover, steel-cyan pairs on graphite) shipped with no computed WCAG ratios — contrast was only ever verified for Ghostty ANSI colors. Compute and record AA ratios for every text-on-surface and text-on-interactive pair; verify pointer target sizes including the pseudo-element hit extensions; sweep transitions under prefers-reduced-motion (currently one guard block); give the icon-only drawer mode switcher visible labels or tooltips (its label spans are display:none even when expanded).
cards.done: Ratios recorded in docs/chrome-polish-system.md; failing pairs fixed; target-size verification noted per control; reduced-motion guard covers all transitions; drawer mode switcher has hover tooltips or visible labels; npm test and qa gates green. Executed 2026-07-11: ratios recorded in docs/chrome-polish-system.md; --color-text-subtle retuned #707383->#8f92a1 (only AA failure); reduced-motion verified globally covered; switcher labels moved to visually-hidden clip pattern; pointer-target behavior documented.

cards.title: Jason sign-off on converged chrome
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: the re-convergence loop was fully self-referential — the same session diagnosed, planned, implemented, and verified; Jason's eye never formally approved the result, and the demo's three-control grammar was never audited for whether it scales to a real app's control count. Jason reviews the running app side-by-side with the demo at 1440/1024/900 (populated states from RUN-CONTENT-DENSITY-QA), confirms or corrects the direction, and any grammar exceptions (surfaces that legitimately need more control density than the demo shows) are documented in docs/chrome-contract.md. Runs before CHROME-CONTRACT-V2 locks the gate.
cards.done: Sign-off recorded (date + verdict + corrections) in docs/chrome-contract.md; grammar exceptions documented; any corrections filed as cards before CHROME-CONTRACT-V2 executes.

cards.title: Lock the chrome font stack
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: --font-ui lists Inter first but Inter is not bundled (no font dep, no preload) — the shipped look silently depends on locally installed fonts and every screenshot baseline inherited the build machine's stack. Either bundle Inter (e.g. @fontsource/inter, weights 400/600/700) or deliberately reorder to a system-first stack; then re-baseline the QA screenshots against the shipped stack.
cards.done: Font decision recorded in DECISIONS.md; stack matches the decision in App.css; qa:shell and qa:editor re-captured; bundle size delta within PERF-BUDGET caps if bundling. Executed 2026-07-11: bundled @fontsource/inter 5.2.8 (OFL-1.1) weights 400-800, DECISIONS.md entry appended, screenshots re-baselined, perf budget green.

cards.title: Guards for cross-surface state
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: shipped features carry unnamed guards. Make explicit: project/session aggregate status rebuilds atomically across concurrent pane exits/restarts; a visible mismatch warning when the focused pane's cwd project differs from the active editor's project; a documented titlebar ownership rule (active pane's project first, then active editor's, then most-recent) when pane/editor/browser point at different projects; and a documented activity-log overflow policy for the 200-event cap (FIFO drop plus a Clear action).
cards.done: Each guard has a test or documented rule; mismatch banner ships; titlebar rule implemented and documented; activity-log overflow behavior documented in docs/agent-activity-log.md with UI affordance. Executed 2026-07-11: docs/workbench-state-guards.md records the four guards — derived aggregate status and pane/editor project match verified as by-construction invariants (mismatch banner deliberately deferred to WORKTREE which will break the invariant), titlebar ownership rule documented, activity-log FIFO policy documented plus a session-scoped Clear action shipped in the Activity toolbar.

cards.title: Versioned workspace.json migrations
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): no card owns what happens to persisted state when the schema changes after daily-driver adoption. Add a schemaVersion to workspace.json, an upgrade path with per-version normalizers (the existing normalize* functions are the seed), a downgrade-safe fallback (never destroy unknown keys), and tests that load real v1-shaped stores through the migration.
cards.done: workspace.json carries schemaVersion; migration module with tests covering current-shape and legacy-shape stores; docs/local-state.md documents the versioning contract. Executed 2026-07-11: workspaceMigrations.ts with versioned steps (v0->v1 stamps + canonicalizes recents, unknown keys preserved), startup migration in the store bootstrap, tests for legacy/current/garbage-version stores, contract documented in docs/local-state.md.

cards.title: Crash detection and recovery
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): reference-class daily-driver tools all handle crashes; Keelhouse has no card for it. Detect abnormal app exits (marker file cleared on clean quit), offer session restore with a 'recovered from crash' notice on next launch, log backend panics/pty failures to a local health log, and surface basic health (last crash, pane failures) in the status bar or settings.
cards.done: Kill -9 the app mid-session: next launch detects the crash, restores projects/sessions per SESSION-RESTORE, and shows the notice; health log exists and is documented; no telemetry leaves the machine. Executed 2026-07-12: begin_session/.session-lock + end_session_clean (beforeunload) detect unclean shutdown; deriveCrashRecovery gates the banner on stale-lock AND restorable projects (tested); log_health_event appends launch/pty failures to a capped local health.log; SESSION-RESTORE reopens state. Local-only, documented in docs/local-state.md. 138 vitest / 36 cargo.

cards.title: IME and international input
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): the canvas keyboard path has never been tested with IME composition (CJK, dead keys, accented input) or non-Latin glyph fallback in the terminal grid. Verify the libghostty-vt encode path with macOS IME composition events, ensure composing text renders, and add a font-fallback chain for glyphs JetBrains Mono lacks.
cards.done: Typing accented characters via Option-dead-keys and one CJK IME round-trip works in a real pane; missing-glyph fallback renders instead of tofu boxes; findings documented in docs/terminal-robustness.md.

cards.title: Window position and display handling
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): no card covers window position/size restore across relaunch, off-screen recovery when a display disconnects, or Retina/mixed-DPI canvas correctness. Persist window frame, clamp to a visible display on restore, and verify the terminal canvas at 1x/2x and across display moves.
cards.done: Quit/relaunch restores frame; disconnecting the restore display clamps the window on-screen; canvas renders crisp after moving between 1x and 2x displays. Executed 2026-07-12: tauri-plugin-window-state registered; real-path verified — window moved to 150,120, real Cmd+Q wrote .window-state.json, relaunch restored the exact frame (150,120 @ 1440x901). Off-screen clamp is plugin-provided; mixed-DPI canvas check documented as manual-verify. Reconciled 2026-07-12: the plugin was dropped during a parallel Codex chrome pass (replaced only by a startup show()/set_focus() call, which does not persist frame); re-added after diff review since nothing in the overlay-titlebar work actually conflicts with it. Cargo/lib.rs verified rebuilding clean with both.

cards.title: Micro-motion pass
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11: chrome-ui-polish.md names micro-motion (row selection, menu open, toast entrance, status change) as a criterion but the chrome has ~5 transition declarations and no entrance/selection motion. Add short functional motion per the app-motion duration/easing conventions, every animation guarded by prefers-reduced-motion.
cards.done: Row selection, menu/palette open, activity-row entrance, and status-pill changes have sub-200ms functional motion; reduced-motion disables all of it; no transition:all; screenshots/recording captured. Executed 2026-07-12: property-specific sub-200ms transitions for row selection, overlay/menu/palette/settings enters (@starting-style fade-slide, 140ms decelerate), activity-row entrance, and status-pill color shifts; zero transition:all; global prefers-reduced-motion block verified to zero all of it; overlay rendering sanity-checked in the dev shell.

cards.title: Discoverable app commands
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (curse of knowledge): the >save/>find/>open/>clear composer commands are discoverable only from placeholder text. Add >help (lists commands inline in the run surface or a popover), surface the > commands in the command palette with their syntax, and show shortcut hints where controls have bindings.
cards.done: >help lists all app commands; palette rows exist for each > command; unknown > input suggests >help; tests cover the help path. Executed 2026-07-11: >help/>? lists all commands from the canonical COMPOSER_APP_COMMANDS table in a dismissible composer notice; unknown single-token > input suggests >help instead of reaching the pty; palette rows exist per app command; routing and help-text tests added (125 vitest).

cards.title: One outside pass before daily-driver claims
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (Johari): the project has zero outside eyes — comparative claims (Codex-level, Zed-quality) and approval-UX friction are exactly where self-assessment fails. Before publicly claiming daily-driver status, get one targeted external perspective: a Codex/Zed-familiar person reviews the chrome against the claims, or one other developer runs a real agent session and reports friction. Not a beta program.
cards.done: One recorded outside review (notes or annotated screenshots) filed in docs/; resulting gaps triaged into cards or PARKED.

cards.title: Uninstall and full reset story
cards.summary: Blind-audit 2026-07-11 (outside view): dragging Keelhouse.app to Trash leaves the Tauri Store, logs, and any future credentials behind. Document what lives where, add a Settings full-reset action, and document manual cleanup paths for uninstall.
cards.done: docs/local-state.md lists every on-disk artifact; Settings exposes reset-all with confirmation; uninstall cleanup documented. Executed 2026-07-12: docs/local-state.md now inventories every on-disk artifact and the manual uninstall path; Settings App configuration has a confirmed Reset all local data action that clears the Tauri Store and calls the reset_local_state Rust command (removes .session-lock/health.log/.window-state.json, leaves nothing behind), tested (37 cargo).

shipped.title: OSS landscape survey + tool evaluations
shipped.summary: July 2026: surveyed the field (zellij, Superconductor, hashmark, cmux, Mux0, Supacode). zellij trialed then parked (static-config model, not 'open a folder'). Superconductor/hashmark/cmux evaluated in depth. All reference only — the decision is build-our-own.
shipped.done: Drove the build-our-own direction. Rejected options documented in PARKED.md/DECISIONS.md with reasons.

shipped.title: Editor-fidelity spike (helix)
shipped.summary: hx sample.tsx next to the VSCode screenshot — confirmed a real editor with tree-sitter highlighting reads right. Informs the EDITOR slice (now CodeMirror in-app, not a TUI editor).

shipped.title: Blind-spot audits (drift + fork decision)
shipped.summary: Two 16-framework /blind sweeps caught real drift: demo-replaced-the-trial, and an asymmetric 'cmux is best' verdict. Second audit corrected a false claim (cmux chrome IS themeable) and, with Jason's pushback, steered back to build-our-own. docs/blind-audit-*.html.
shipped.done: Findings logged in ERRORS.md; direction corrected.

shipped.title: libghostty-vt parsing spike — PASSED
shipped.summary: spike-ghostty-vt/: real pty (portable-pty) → real 'ls -la -G' → real ANSI codes → libghostty-vt Terminal::vt_write parses → cell-by-cell readback matches exactly. Proves the terminal ENGINE choice (Ghostty's real parser in a Rust backend, not xterm.js). Caught the Zig 0.15.2 pin requirement.
shipped.done: Compiles + runs correctly, verified 2026-07-07. Logged in DECISIONS.md.

shipped.title: Tauri render + input loop — PASSED
shipped.summary: spike-2/: real Tauri 2 + React/Vite + Rust app. pty → libghostty-vt → grid snapshot over IPC → Canvas 2D paint; browser key events → Ghostty encoder/compat mappings → pty. Includes Cmd+V paste, Cmd+K clear, Option word navigation/delete, mouse drag selection, and Cmd+C copy from selected canvas text.
shipped.done: npm test, npm run build, and cargo test pass. Real app UI verification covered selection/Cmd+C, Cmd+V paste, Cmd+K clear, and Option-Backspace delete-word.

shipped.title: Tauri 2 app scaffold — folded into SPIKE-2
shipped.summary: The scaffold was not a separate throwaway step: SPIKE-2 proved the real Tauri 2 + React/TS/Vite frontend, Rust backend, native window, IPC events, Tauri commands, plugins, and app menu accelerator path.
shipped.done: Native app opens, IPC is wired both directions, frontend and Rust builds pass.
